# pub title
title: "Characterising Gaming Group Experiences"

# publication image
image:
  name: 2026_04_chi_gaming_group_experiences.jpg
  alt-text: "The figure shows nested circles representing three levels of social structures in gaming. The largest circle is labelled 'Communities', which are described as having low stability, low interaction, and loose ties. Within it, a medium circle labelled 'Extended Circle' represents groups with moderate stability, occasional interaction, and some closeness. Inside and overlapping within these are several smaller circles labelled 'Core Groups', which are characterised by higher stability, frequent interaction, and closer ties. The figure illustrates how these gaming groups exist as more stable and tightly connected clusters within broader, less stable communities." # provide a short description for the image #a11y

# short description of the publication
description: "Gaming groups are a prevalent and impactful part of multiplayer gaming, yet current research predominantly centers on the individual player experience. In this paper, we argue that understanding the characteristics and dynamics of these groups is necessary to design systems that intentionally support complex and positive social interactions. To explore this, we interviewed 15 long-term players about their lived experiences, examining group lifecycles, how group dynamics impact gameplay, and how current games and platforms either support or constrain these relationships. Our findings show that gaming groups are complex and continually evolve in size, composition, and focus, resulting in diverse group cultures. These shifting dynamics impact play and introduce requirements that extend beyond the simplistic group features currently provided by most games and tools. We discuss opportunities to reframe player experience evaluation, moving beyond individual metrics to design systems that accommodate the evolving trajectories of gaming groups."

# authors of the publication
authors: Daniel Reis, Kathrin Gerling, André Rodrigues
